symptoms.symptom_name: Firm hyperkeratotic plantar papule, slowly enlarging

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard, rough, slowly growing plantar lump is typical of a wart, but also of a corn/callus — not discriminating on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain on lateral (pinch) compression greater than on direct vertical pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lateral-compression tenderness is the classic bedside discriminator favouring a wart.
A corn typically hurts on direct downward pressure, not on pinching the sides.

symptoms.symptom_name: Barefoot use of communal pool/shower floors (HPV inoculation exposure)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Communal wet-floor exposure is a well-recognised route of plantar HPV acquisition.

symptoms.symptom_name: Disruption of the dermatoglyphic (skin-line) pattern over the lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Warts interrupt the skin lines; corns preserve them. Clinician-observed close inspection — sealed, not available over chat.
Preserved skin lines would favour a corn.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pinpoint thrombosed capillaries (black dots) visible on paring the callus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Thrombosed capillaries revealed on paring are the confirmatory sign — a clinician manoeuvre, sealed and unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no spreading erythema, no fluctuance or discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep soft-tissue abscess / infected foreign body
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic and local infective signs makes a pyogenic collection or cellulitis unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No diabetes and normal foot sensation reported

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lowers the probability of a neuropathic ulcer and permits (with caution) a keratolytic; a positive answer would flip management entirely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Antalgic gait with functional immobility (hopping, furniture-walking)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflects pain severity and fall risk (living alone) but does not point to a specific diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Minimal relief from an over-the-counter corn plaster

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Poor response to a thin corn plaster is common for warts, which usually need paring plus sustained keratolytic therapy; not discriminating.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person paring/debridement of the callus by a clinician or podiatrist (confirms diagnosis and relieves pressure pain)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Paring both confirms the wart (thrombosed capillaries, disrupted skin lines) and provides immediate mechanical pain relief.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Diabetes / neurovascular foot screen before keratolytic therapy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes a neuropathic or ischaemic foot in which salicylic acid would be unsafe.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Dermoscopy or biopsy of an atypical, pigmented, bleeding or non-resolving lesion
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Indicated only if the lesion is atypical or fails to resolve — to exclude amelanotic melanoma; not required for a classic wart.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods, plain radiography or foot ultrasound for a classic plantar wart
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No role in a typical, non-infected plantar wart; ordering them reflects over-investigation.
